Container.Name: obsidian-git-backup
Container.Overview: Syncs an Obsidian vault via obsidian-headless (official Obsidian Sync client) and automatically backs it up to a git repository. Every time Obsidian Sync delivers changes, they are committed and pushed to your git remote — giving you a private, versioned backup of your vault with full history. This container has no web UI and exposes no ports. Monitor it via container logs.
Container.Requires: An Obsidian Sync subscription and a git repository with SSH access. Before installing: (1) On your desktop or laptop (not the Unraid server), install Node.js, then run 'npm install -g obsidian-headless' and 'ob login' to obtain your auth token (requires a web browser). (2) If the git-watcher stops committing on a busy server, you may need to raise the inotify watch limit on the Unraid host: echo 524288 > /proc/sys/fs/inotify/max_user_watches (add to Settings > Boot > Go file to persist across reboots). After first start: check container logs for the generated SSH public key and add it to your git server (GitHub/GitLab) before the first push can succeed.
Container.Changes: ### 2026-03-03 - Initial Unraid CA template release - Continuous Obsidian Sync via obsidian-headless - Filesystem-event-driven git commits (no polling) - Automatic SSH key generation - E2E encrypted vault support

Config.Name: Obsidian Auth Token
Config.Description: Your Obsidian auth token from 'ob login'. Grants read access to all your Obsidian Sync vaults — treat like a password.

Config.Name: Vault Name
Config.Description: The name of your Obsidian vault exactly as shown in the Obsidian app (case-sensitive). Run 'ob sync-list-remote' to list available vaults.

Config.Name: Git Remote URL
Config.Description: SSH URL of the git remote to push to. Example: git@github.com:username/vault-backup.git

Config.Name: E2EE Password
Config.Description: Only needed if the vault uses E2E encryption. Your git repository will contain plaintext notes — secure your git remote accordingly.

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Stores SSH keys and obsidian-headless device state. Must survive restarts.

Config.Name: Vault Storage
Config.Description: Vault data and git working tree. Without this, the container re-clones the entire vault on every restart.

Config.Name: Git Branch
Config.Description: Git branch to push to. Must already exist on the remote.

Config.Name: Debounce Seconds
Config.Description: Seconds of filesystem quiet before committing. For large vaults, consider 60-120.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: Leave at 99 unless you know you need a different UID.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Leave at 100 unless you know you need a different GID.
